prompt:
You are extracting atomic facts from a topic document.

The topic document below contains all source text for one specific topic, assembled with provenance headings. Your output must pass all Five Tests.

**Test 1 — Stand-Alone**: A reader with no other facts must fully understand the policy. No implied "who does this apply to?", "when does it apply?", "except what?". Include the subject explicitly in every fact.

**Test 2 — One Topic**: A single noun phrase names the subject. If "and" is needed to name the subject, split into two facts.

**Test 3 — Reconstruction**: Concatenating all your facts must be informationally equivalent to the topic document: same obligations, permissions, exceptions, numerical values. No information omitted, none added.

**Test 4 — Canonical Form**: Active voice. No document-navigation language ("as mentioned in", "refer to", "see above", "this section"). No redundant preamble ("it is important to note that", "this policy states", "please be aware").

**Test 5 — Template-Slot Ready**: No audience-specific framing baked in ("as a parent, you should…", "staff are reminded that…"). No channel-specific framing ("as you read this handbook…").

Instructions:
- This is the only pass that writes content — rewrite source text into canonical form
- Eliminate redundancy across provenance spans (same obligation stated in multiple spans → one fact)
- Split only at true topic boundaries (when the subject noun phrase changes)
- `title`: short noun phrase naming the subject of this fact
- `kind`: assign ONLY for clearly non-policy content — 'rationale', 'principle', or 'context'. Leave null for all policy facts (rules, procedures, fees, contact details, definitions)
- `topic_tags`: kebab-case tags, consistent with the topic_slug used in grouping

Audience scope: parents
Channel scope: parent-handbook

Topic document:
### School Hours and Attendance > School Hours, Punctuality and Drop-Off / Pick-Up

**LATE PICK-UPS** — If families arrive after 4:55 p.m., a late pick-up fee applies:

*   From 4:55 p.m. to 5:15 p.m. — €5 per late pick-up
*   From 5:16 p.m. to 5:30 p.m. — €10 per late pick-up
*   From 5:31 p.m. to 5:45 p.m. — €20 per late pick-up
raw_response: {"facts":[{"title":"Late pick-up fee for 4:55 p.m. to 5:15 p.m.","content":"Families are charged a late pick-up fee of €5 if they arrive between 4:55 p.m. and 5:15 p.m. to collect their children from school.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["school-hours","late-pick-ups"]},{"title":"Late pick-up fee for 5:16 p.m. to 5:30 p.m.","content":"Families are charged a late pick-up fee of €10 if they arrive between 5:16 p.m. and 5:30 p.m. to collect their children from school.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["school-hours","late-pick-ups"]},{"title":"Late pick-up fee for 5:31 p.m. to 5:45 p.m.","content":"Families are charged a late pick-up fee of €20 if they arrive between 5:31 p.m. and 5:45 p.m. to collect their children from school.","kind":null,"topic_tags":["school-hours","late-pick-ups"]}]}

facts.content: Families are charged a late pick-up fee of €5 if they arrive between 4:55 p.m. and 5:15 p.m. to collect their children from school.

facts.title: Late pick-up fee for 5:16 p.m. to 5:30 p.m.
facts.content: Families are charged a late pick-up fee of €10 if they arrive between 5:16 p.m. and 5:30 p.m. to collect their children from school.

facts.title: Late pick-up fee for 5:31 p.m. to 5:45 p.m.
facts.content: Families are charged a late pick-up fee of €20 if they arrive between 5:31 p.m. and 5:45 p.m. to collect their children from school.
